title: Job Openings at Bonzee Consulting
content:
Bonzee Consulting is a forward-thinking and people-oriented HR/Business Advisory company.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Business Development Manager

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
Business Development & Revenue Growth (Retail & B2B):
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Identify, develop, and close new retail and B2B business opportunities within the advertising and marketing space.
	Develop and execute strategic business development plans to increase market share.
	Generate leads, conduct prospect meetings, and convert opportunities into long-term partnerships.
	Achieve revenue targets and oversee the company’s sales pipeline performance.


Client Relationship Management:


	Build and maintain strong relationships with key retail and corporate (B2B) clients.
	Lead high-level negotiations and contract discussions.
	Ensure exceptional customer experience and client satisfaction.
	Identify upselling and cross-selling opportunities within existing accounts.


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 per month.

Application Closing Date: 31st March, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

 

 

2.) Network Engineer

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Contract

Responsibilities


	Design, configure, and troubleshoot MikroTik routers, switches, and wireless solutions.
	Implement and maintain VLANs, bridging, firewall rules, NAT, QoS, and traffic shaping.
	Set up and manage VPNs (IPsec, WireGuard, SSTP, L2TP, OpenVPN) with secure routing.
	Perform network monitoring, performance optimization, and rapid issue resolution.
	Support LAN/WAN infrastructure, OSPF/BGP routing, Hotspot, and user management.
	Document all network configurations and changes for easy reference, audits, and disaster recovery.
	Assist in network capacity planning and expansion, recommending upgrades or enhancements as needed.
	Collaborate with IT and other departments to ensure network solutions meet organizational needs.
	Provide technical support and guidance to team members or clients on network-related issues.
	Stay updated on emerging networking technologies and MikroTik features to implement best practices and improve network efficiency.


Application Closing Date: 31st March, 2026.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

 

 

3.) Service Manager

Job Location: Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Over see operations
	Supports ceo with strategy
	Business Development
	Drive sales
	Facility Manager.


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 / month

Application Closing Date: 28th March, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

     

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "Fresh2Go Smoothie &amp; Co. is a fast growing food and beverage brand focused on delivering fresh, tasty, and affordable meals and drinks. We operate multiple outlets and specialize in smoothies, milkshakes, sandwiches, shawarma, parfaits, burgers, and other ready to eat meals made with quality ingredients.

Our company is driven by a strong commitment to hygiene, customer satisfaction, and operational excellence. We create a dynamic and supportive work environment where teamwork, innovation, and personal growth are encouraged. At Fresh2Go, employees are valued as key contributors to our success, and we are passionate about building a brand that customers trust and talents are proud to be part of.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Production Assistant / Outlet Attendant
Job Location:&nbsp;Ibadan, Alakia - Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Duties


	Preparation of customers&#39; orders.
	Assisting in checking stocks and informing the supervisor when supplies are low
	Interact politely with customers when necessary and maintain professionalism
	Following the safety and hygiene requirements of the company
	Cleaning &amp; sanitizing the food preparation area.
	Taking stock of food materials


Requirements


	Minimum education qualification: NCE, OND, HND
	Relevant work experience
	Must be responsible, neat, and presentable
	Reliable and efficient.


Salary
N60,000 - N80,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;oluwatomisin668@gmail.com&nbsp;using &quot;Production Assistant&quot; as the subject of the mail.",

Production Assistant / Outlet Attendant Job at Fresh2Go Smoothie & Co.




        
    

        
        Fresh2Go Smoothie & Co. is a fast growing food and beverage brand focused on delivering fresh, tasty, and affordable meals and drinks. We operate multiple outlets and specialize in smoothies, milkshakes, sandwiches, shawarma, parfaits, burgers, and other ready to eat meals made with quality ingredients.

Our company is driven by a strong commitment to hygiene, customer satisfaction, and operational excellence. We create a dynamic and supportive work environment where teamwork, innovation, and personal growth are encouraged. At Fresh2Go, employees are valued as key contributors to our success, and we are passionate about building a brand that customers trust and talents are proud to be part of.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Production Assistant / Outlet Attendant
Job Location: Ibadan, Alakia - Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Duties


	Preparation of customers' orders.
	Assisting in checking stocks and informing the supervisor when supplies are low
	Interact politely with customers when necessary and maintain professionalism
	Following the safety and hygiene requirements of the company
	Cleaning & sanitizing the food preparation area.
	Taking stock of food materials


Requirements


	Minimum education qualification: NCE, OND, HND
	Relevant work experience
	Must be responsible, neat, and presentable
	Reliable and efficient.


Salary
N60,000 - N80,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Production Assistant" as the subject of the mail.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Head of Internal Control / Audit",
  "description": "Reliable Plastic was incorporated on April 28th, 1998. We have been committed to providing the best quality products to our customers since its inception. Apart from the outstanding service that is provided, consumers are sure to be completely satisfied with the end product. Reliable Plastic promises to use only quality material for your product. Everything is done in-house with the company and therefore is your one-stop shop for product manufacturing.

The difference is our service! We stand behind our products and provide the best, most comprehensive ongoing customer service. We take the time to learn about your needs and work with you to provide the best fastening solutions for your specific application and welcome orders requiring our custom fabrication services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head of Internal Control / Audit

Job Location:&nbsp;Onitsha,&nbsp; Anambra
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Head of Internal Control is responsible for designing, implementing, and monitoring internal control systems to safeguard company assets, ensure compliance with policies and regulations, and improve operational efficiency.
	The role requires strong oversight of financial and operational processes, particularly within a manufacturing environment, to mitigate risks and prevent fraud, waste, and inefficiencies.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop, implement, and continuously improve internal control frameworks, policies, and procedures across all departments.
	Conduct periodic audits (financial, operational, and compliance) to ensure adherence to company policies and regulatory requirements.
	Evaluate manufacturing processes to identify risks, inefficiencies, and control gaps.
	Monitor inventory management, production processes, procurement activities, and warehouse operations to prevent losses and discrepancies.
	Ensure proper documentation and authorization of transactions across all business units.
	Lead investigations into suspected fraud, misconduct, or internal control breaches and recommend corrective actions.
	Provide detailed audit reports with actionable recommendations to management.
	Collaborate with department heads to ensure implementation of corrective actions and process improvements.
	Review and validate financial records, cost controls, and expense management systems.
	Ensure compliance with statutory regulations, company policies, and industry best practices.
	Supervise and mentor internal control/audit team members.
	Establish risk management strategies and maintain a risk register for the organization.
	Support external auditors during statutory audits and regulatory inspections.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Reduction in operational losses and control breaches
	Effectiveness of internal control systems
	Timely completion of audit assignments
	Implementation rate of audit recommendations
	Compliance level across departments
	Accuracy of inventory and financial records


Qualifications &amp; Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Accounting, Finance or a related field.
	5&ndash;10 years ofexperience in Internal Control, Audit, or Risk Management, preferably within a manufacturing company.
	Professional certification such as ACA, ACCA, CIA, CISA, or ICAN is an added advantage.
	Strong knowledge of internal control frameworks, audit procedures, and risk management practices.
	Solid understanding of manufacturing processes, inventory control, and cost management.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and accounting/audit software.


Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
	High level of integrity and attention to detail
	Excellent leadership and team management abilities
	Strong communication and report-writing skills
	Ability to work independently and enforce compliance
	Critical thinking and decision-making capability
	Strong understanding of fraud detection and prevention techniques


Personal Attributes:


	Ethical and highly trustworthy
	Assertive with strong enforcement capability
	Detail-oriented and result-driven
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines
	Proactive and highly observant


Working Conditions:


	Primarily office-based with frequent visits to factory floors, warehouses, and operational units
	May require extended hours during audits or investigations.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV/Resume to:&nbsp;reliablesteelplasticrecruit@gmail.com&nbsp;using &ldquo;Application for Head of Internal control / Audit.&rdquo; as the subject of the mail.",
  "datePosted": "2026-03-26",
  "validThrough": "2026-04-12",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "RELIABLE STEEL AND PLASTIC IND. LTD"
    
  },
  "jobLocation": {

Head of Internal Control / Audit Job at Reliable Steel and Plastic Industrial Limited




        
    

        
        Reliable Plastic was incorporated on April 28th, 1998. We have been committed to providing the best quality products to our customers since its inception. Apart from the outstanding service that is provided, consumers are sure to be completely satisfied with the end product. Reliable Plastic promises to use only quality material for your product. Everything is done in-house with the company and therefore is your one-stop shop for product manufacturing.

The difference is our service! We stand behind our products and provide the best, most comprehensive ongoing customer service. We take the time to learn about your needs and work with you to provide the best fastening solutions for your specific application and welcome orders requiring our custom fabrication services.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head of Internal Control / Audit

Job Location: Onitsha,  Anambra
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Head of Internal Control is responsible for designing, implementing, and monitoring internal control systems to safeguard company assets, ensure compliance with policies and regulations, and improve operational efficiency.
	The role requires strong oversight of financial and operational processes, particularly within a manufacturing environment, to mitigate risks and prevent fraud, waste, and inefficiencies.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop, implement, and continuously improve internal control frameworks, policies, and procedures across all departments.
	Conduct periodic audits (financial, operational, and compliance) to ensure adherence to company policies and regulatory requirements.
	Evaluate manufacturing processes to identify risks, inefficiencies, and control gaps.
	Monitor inventory management, production processes, procurement activities, and warehouse operations to prevent losses and discrepancies.
	Ensure proper documentation and authorization of transactions across all business units.
	Lead investigations into suspected fraud, misconduct, or internal control breaches and recommend corrective actions.
	Provide detailed audit reports with actionable recommendations to management.
	Collaborate with department heads to ensure implementation of corrective actions and process improvements.
	Review and validate financial records, cost controls, and expense management systems.
	Ensure compliance with statutory regulations, company policies, and industry best practices.
	Supervise and mentor internal control/audit team members.
	Establish risk management strategies and maintain a risk register for the organization.
	Support external auditors during statutory audits and regulatory inspections.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Reduction in operational losses and control breaches
	Effectiveness of internal control systems
	Timely completion of audit assignments
	Implementation rate of audit recommendations
	Compliance level across departments
	Accuracy of inventory and financial records


Qualifications & Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance or a related field.
	5–10 years ofexperience in Internal Control, Audit, or Risk Management, preferably within a manufacturing company.
	Professional certification such as ACA, ACCA, CIA, CISA, or ICAN is an added advantage.
	Strong knowledge of internal control frameworks, audit procedures, and risk management practices.
	Solid understanding of manufacturing processes, inventory control, and cost management.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and accounting/audit software.


Skills & Competencies:


	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
	High level of integrity and attention to detail
	Excellent leadership and team management abilities
	Strong communication and report-writing skills
	Ability to work independently and enforce compliance
	Critical thinking and decision-making capability
	Strong understanding of fraud detection and prevention techniques


Personal Attributes:


	Ethical and highly trustworthy
	Assertive with strong enforcement capability
	Detail-oriented and result-driven
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines
	Proactive and highly observant


Working Conditions:


	Primarily office-based with frequent visits to factory floors, warehouses, and operational units
	May require extended hours during audits or investigations.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV/Resume to: [email protected] using “Application for Head of Internal control / Audit.” as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Facility / Maintenance Assistant",
  "description": "Naji Realties is a leading hospitality and real estate company in Nigeria, dedicated to providing exceptional services and experiences that exceed our customers&#39; expectations. With a strong presence in Abuja and Lagos, our experts are passionate about delivering top-notch solutions in property development, management, and hospitality.

If you share our values and passion for excellence, we invite you to explore career opportunities with us. At Naji Realties, we foster a culture of growth, learning, and collaboration. Be part of our dynamic team and help us shape the future of hospitality and real estate in Nigeria

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Facility / Maintenance Assistant

Job Location:&nbsp;Utako, Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	The Facility / Maintenance Assistant is responsible for supporting the upkeep, safety, and functionality of the organization&rsquo;s facilities.
	The role involves carrying out routine maintenance, handling repairs, and ensuring that all equipment and infrastructure are in good working condition to support smooth daily operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Perform routine inspection of facilities to identify maintenance needs
	Carry out basic repairs and maintenance of electrical, plumbing, and general fixtures
	Ensure all equipment and facilities are functioning properly and report major faults promptly
	Maintain cleanliness and orderliness of facility areas where required
	Monitor and manage maintenance schedules for equipment and infrastructure
	Assist in coordinating external technicians and service providers when necessary
	Keep records of maintenance activities, repairs, and equipment servicing
	Ensure compliance with health and safety standards within the facility
	Respond promptly to maintenance requests and emergencies
	Support setup and maintenance of office spaces, workstations, and common areas
	Assist guests when necessary.


Requirements


	Minimum of OND or relevant technical certification
	2-3 years of experience in facility management or maintenance roles
	Basic knowledge of electrical, plumbing, and general building maintenance
	Ability to troubleshoot and carry out minor repairs independently
	Good physical condition and ability to handle manual tasks
	Strong attention to detail and proactive approach to maintenance
	Ability to work with minimum supervision
	Good communication and teamwork skills
	High level of responsibility, reliability, and discipline
	Neat appearance and professional conduct.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should their CV to:&nbsp;careers@najirealties.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Facility / Maintenance Assistant Job at Naji Realties




        
    

        
        Naji Realties is a leading hospitality and real estate company in Nigeria, dedicated to providing exceptional services and experiences that exceed our customers' expectations. With a strong presence in Abuja and Lagos, our experts are passionate about delivering top-notch solutions in property development, management, and hospitality.

If you share our values and passion for excellence, we invite you to explore career opportunities with us. At Naji Realties, we foster a culture of growth, learning, and collaboration. Be part of our dynamic team and help us shape the future of hospitality and real estate in Nigeria

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Facility / Maintenance Assistant
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Utako, Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	The Facility / Maintenance Assistant is responsible for supporting the upkeep, safety, and functionality of the organization’s facilities.
	The role involves carrying out routine maintenance, handling repairs, and ensuring that all equipment and infrastructure are in good working condition to support smooth daily operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Perform routine inspection of facilities to identify maintenance needs
	Carry out basic repairs and maintenance of electrical, plumbing, and general fixtures
	Ensure all equipment and facilities are functioning properly and report major faults promptly
	Maintain cleanliness and orderliness of facility areas where required
	Monitor and manage maintenance schedules for equipment and infrastructure
	Assist in coordinating external technicians and service providers when necessary
	Keep records of maintenance activities, repairs, and equipment servicing
	Ensure compliance with health and safety standards within the facility
	Respond promptly to maintenance requests and emergencies
	Support setup and maintenance of office spaces, workstations, and common areas
	Assist guests when necessary.


Requirements


	Minimum of OND or relevant technical certification
	2-3 years of experience in facility management or maintenance roles
	Basic knowledge of electrical, plumbing, and general building maintenance
	Ability to troubleshoot and carry out minor repairs independently
	Good physical condition and ability to handle manual tasks
	Strong attention to detail and proactive approach to maintenance
	Ability to work with minimum supervision
	Good communication and teamwork skills
	High level of responsibility, reliability, and discipline
	Neat appearance and professional conduct.


Method of Application

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Visual Brand Designer",
  "description": "Sahara Group is a leading international energy and infrastructure conglomerate with operations in over 38 countries across Africa, Middle East, Europe and Asia. Spanning three decades, we have broken ground and challenged stereotypes across the global business landscape. The women and men who make up our organization are our strongest levers for growth.

We recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Visual Brand Designer

Job Identification: 537
Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos

Role Accountabilities
Brand Visual Identity Development:


	Evolve and maintain Sahara&rsquo;s visual identity system, ensuring a modern, cohesive, and globally appealing brand expression.
	Develop design guides, component libraries, and visual standards for use across all business units and regions.
	Ensure visual consistency across print, digital, video, and experiential platforms.


Creative Design &amp; Layout Execution:


	Produce high-quality designs for publications, brochures, corporate reports, internal communications, presentations, infographics, and campaign visuals.
	Create clean, structured layouts that apply strong visual hierarchy and understand the flow of information.
	Adapt complex technical information into simple, visually compelling formats.


Digital &amp; UI Design Support:


	Translate the Sahara visual language across digital platforms, supporting website elements, landing pages, microsites, and intranet visual assets.
	Work with the Digital Web Experience Manager to ensure brand alignment across user interface components.


Motion &amp; Multimedia Design:


	Design and animate motion graphics for intros, transitions, lower thirds, logo stings, brand elements, and video assets.
	Develop animated visual systems that enhance Sahara&rsquo;s digital storytelling and modern brand posture.
	Support video editing teams with stylistic overlays, graphic inserts, and brand-based visual enhancements.


Social Media &amp; Campaign Design:


	Create visually strong content for social platforms and digital campaigns that elevate brand perception.
	Define the visual tone for major communication moments, anniversaries, product launches, and internal campaigns.
	Work cross-functionally to ensure every campaign is aligned to the brand&rsquo;s evolving global style.


Brand Governance and Quality Control:


	Review and approve internal and external design outputs to enforce brand consistency.
	Maintain asset libraries, templates, and design repositories for organisation-wide use.
	Provide creative direction to vendors, printers, and production agencies as needed.


Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Visual Communication, Digital Design, or relevant discipline
	2&ndash;5 years of professional design experience in a corporate, agency, or emerging brand environment
	Portfolio demonstrating brand identity, layout work, multimedia design, and motion graphics.


Technical Competencies:


	Advanced proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign, After Effects) and Figma.
	Strong grounding in visual hierarchy, typography, color theory, and composition.
	Experience in brand identity development, logo design, and visual system creation.
	Ability to design for both print and digital platforms, including UI support for web assets.
	Competence in motion design, vector animation, and creation of animated brand elements.
	Basic understanding of digital formats, rendering, compression, and exporting for multi-channel use.


Behavioral &amp; Creative Competencies:


	Strong creative intuition with the ability to modernize and evolve established brand systems.
	Excellent attention to detail and a commitment to design excellence.
	Ability to manage multiple design requests, prioritise effectively, and deliver on tight timelines.
	Collaborative mindset with strong communication skills and the ability to present design concepts clearly.
	Curiosity for emerging design trends, technologies, and global aesthetic standards.
",

  "validThrough": "2026-04-19",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

Visual Brand Designer Job at Sahara Group




        
    

        
        Sahara Group is a leading international energy and infrastructure conglomerate with operations in over 38 countries across Africa, Middle East, Europe and Asia. Spanning three decades, we have broken ground and challenged stereotypes across the global business landscape. The women and men who make up our organization are our strongest levers for growth.

We recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Visual Brand Designer

Job Identification: 537
Job Location: Lagos

Role Accountabilities
Brand Visual Identity Development:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Evolve and maintain Sahara’s visual identity system, ensuring a modern, cohesive, and globally appealing brand expression.
	Develop design guides, component libraries, and visual standards for use across all business units and regions.
	Ensure visual consistency across print, digital, video, and experiential platforms.


Creative Design & Layout Execution:


	Produce high-quality designs for publications, brochures, corporate reports, internal communications, presentations, infographics, and campaign visuals.
	Create clean, structured layouts that apply strong visual hierarchy and understand the flow of information.
	Adapt complex technical information into simple, visually compelling formats.


Digital & UI Design Support:


	Translate the Sahara visual language across digital platforms, supporting website elements, landing pages, microsites, and intranet visual assets.
	Work with the Digital Web Experience Manager to ensure brand alignment across user interface components.


Motion & Multimedia Design:


	Design and animate motion graphics for intros, transitions, lower thirds, logo stings, brand elements, and video assets.
	Develop animated visual systems that enhance Sahara’s digital storytelling and modern brand posture.
	Support video editing teams with stylistic overlays, graphic inserts, and brand-based visual enhancements.


Social Media & Campaign Design:


	Create visually strong content for social platforms and digital campaigns that elevate brand perception.
	Define the visual tone for major communication moments, anniversaries, product launches, and internal campaigns.
	Work cross-functionally to ensure every campaign is aligned to the brand’s evolving global style.


Brand Governance and Quality Control:


	Review and approve internal and external design outputs to enforce brand consistency.
	Maintain asset libraries, templates, and design repositories for organisation-wide use.
	Provide creative direction to vendors, printers, and production agencies as needed.


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Visual Communication, Digital Design, or relevant discipline
	2–5 years of professional design experience in a corporate, agency, or emerging brand environment
	Portfolio demonstrating brand identity, layout work, multimedia design, and motion graphics.


Technical Competencies:


	Advanced proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign, After Effects) and Figma.
	Strong grounding in visual hierarchy, typography, color theory, and composition.
	Experience in brand identity development, logo design, and visual system creation.
	Ability to design for both print and digital platforms, including UI support for web assets.
	Competence in motion design, vector animation, and creation of animated brand elements.
	Basic understanding of digital formats, rendering, compression, and exporting for multi-channel use.


Behavioral & Creative Competencies:


	Strong creative intuition with the ability to modernize and evolve established brand systems.
	Excellent attention to detail and a commitment to design excellence.
	Ability to manage multiple design requests, prioritise effectively, and deliver on tight timelines.
	Collaborative mindset with strong communication skills and the ability to present design concepts clearly.
	Curiosity for emerging design trends, technologies, and global aesthetic standards.
